skill.name: marketing-demand-acquisition
skill.description: Plan B2B SaaS demand generation, paid media, SEO, partnerships, attribution, and CAC analysis for Series A teams.
skill.summary: Build acquisition plans across paid media, SEO, partnerships, attribution, and CAC reporting.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but semantic review found the SKILL.md hits are Markdown examples, marketing terminology, currency symbols, and template text. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, destructive behavior, network calls, shell execution, or weak cryptographic implementation was found. The package includes a local Python CAC calculator, so the residual risk is limited to a small script execution surface.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences, inline code, campaign templates, UTM examples, and advertising structures. They are documentation content and are not executable shell calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows fenced Markdown examples and template text, not interpreter calls. No subprocess, shell, eval, or command construction pattern was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged weak cryptographic algorithm locations contain marketing descriptions, ad copy templates, SEO metadata guidance, UTM tracking discussion, and test planning text. No hashing, cipher, certificate, or cryptography API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are plain prose or advertising templates. They do not define or invoke MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Marketing and Analytics Language
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. System and network reconnaissance detections map to terms such as paid search, campaign structure, website visitors, Google Search Console, and checking SEO issues. These are marketing workflow references, not host or network discovery instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed passages describe acquisition channels and SEO audits. They do not include commands such as whoami, uname, ifconfig, nmap, port scans, or network probing.

low_findings.title: Local CAC Calculator Script Has Limited Execution Surface
low_findings.description: Verdict: LOW_RISK. The package includes a Python CAC calculator. The script uses hardcoded example campaign data, arithmetic functions, and formatted console output; review found no subprocess calls, file writes, network access, environment access, or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Full script review confirms only local calculation and printing behavior. Residual risk exists because users may execute a community-provided script, but no dangerous behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Scaling teams need clear acquisition plans across paid media, SEO, partnerships, and attribution. This skill provides structured playbooks, templates, benchmarks, and CAC analysis guidance.
actual_capabilities: Creates full-funnel campaign briefs for awareness, consideration, and conversion programs.
Provides paid media playbooks for LinkedIn Ads, Google Ads, Meta Ads, and retargeting.
Gives SEO planning templates for technical audits, keyword tiers, and content calendars.
Outlines partnership, affiliate, co-marketing, and HubSpot partner tracking workflows.
Defines attribution dashboards, reporting metrics, handoff protocols, and CAC benchmarks.
Includes a local Python calculator for blended and channel-specific customer acquisition cost.
limitations: It does not connect directly to HubSpot, ad platforms, analytics tools, or partner portals.
Benchmarks are generic B2B SaaS guidance and should be adjusted with company data.
GDPR and consent guidance is high level and is not legal advice.
The CAC calculator uses example data unless users edit it with their own numbers.

use_cases.title: Launch a New Acquisition Program
use_cases.description: Plan campaign objectives, channels, audiences, budgets, offers, and sales handoff rules for a new quarterly program.

use_cases.title: Improve Paid Channel Efficiency
use_cases.description: Review channel mix, targeting, creative tests, bid strategy, CAC targets, and optimization rules across paid platforms.

use_cases.title: Build Partner-Sourced Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create partner tiers, outreach plans, affiliate commissions, co-marketing campaigns, and HubSpot tracking reports.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Campaign Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-day demand generation campaign brief for a B2B SaaS product. Include objective, audience, budget, channels, offer, success metrics, and sales handoff criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Paid Media Mix
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current paid media budget and recommend changes across LinkedIn Ads, Google Search, Google Display, Meta Ads, and partnerships. Use CAC, conversion rate, and pipeline quality as decision factors.

prompt_templates.title: Design SEO Growth Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an SEO acquisition plan for our product category. Include technical audit priorities, keyword tiers, content types by funnel stage, link building tactics, and refresh cadence.

prompt_templates.title: Build Attribution Operating Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a HubSpot reporting and attribution model for a hybrid product-led and sales-led motion. Include funnel stages, dashboards, handoff SLAs, channel dimensions, and executive metrics.

output_examples.input: Plan a LinkedIn ABM campaign for enterprise SaaS buyers.
output_examples.output: A campaign brief with account segments, audience filters, monthly budget, creative angles, lead form guidance, HubSpot setup steps, and SQL handoff rules.

output_examples.input: Help me reduce CAC across paid channels.
output_examples.output: A channel review with CAC benchmarks, budget reallocation advice, bid strategy changes, landing page checks, and a two-week testing plan.

output_examples.input: Create a partner marketing program for our startup.
output_examples.output: A partner tier model with target profiles, outreach messaging, enablement assets, commission guidance, tracking fields, and reporting cadence.

best_practices: Provide current spend, conversion rates, pipeline value, target regions, and sales cycle assumptions before asking for recommendations.
Validate benchmarks against your own CRM, analytics, and finance data before changing budgets.
Define MQL, SQL, opportunity, and customer stages before building attribution reports.
anti_patterns: Do not copy benchmark CAC targets without adjusting for ACV, sales cycle, market, and product maturity.
Do not run paid campaigns without UTM discipline, landing page ownership, and sales follow-up SLAs.
Do not treat SEO, partnerships, and paid media as isolated channels when reporting pipeline impact.

faq.question: What type of marketing team is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is best for B2B SaaS teams planning demand generation, paid acquisition, SEO, partnerships, and attribution.

faq.question: Can it manage live ad campaigns?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning and optimization guidance, but it does not connect to advertising platforms.

faq.question: Does it include HubSpot guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers campaign tracking, lead scoring, attribution reporting, workflows, and partner source tracking.

faq.question: Can it calculate CAC?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance and a local Python calculator for blended and channel-specific CAC.

faq.question: Is it suitable for international expansion?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes EU, US, and Canada planning considerations, including localization and channel mix guidance.

faq.question: Does it replace legal review for privacy compliance?
faq.answer: No. Its privacy notes are general operational guidance and should be reviewed by qualified legal counsel.
